items.title: ‘Everything I know about evil I learned in that small town’: John Carpenter on terrifying the world with Halloween, The Thing and The Fog
items.summary: At 78, the king of horror and sci-fi movies is as much of a rebel as ever. He talks about creativity, compromise (he’s not a fan) and how he became addicted to moviemakingIn 2024, John Carpenter had a dream that sounds like one of his movies: it involved a portal to the underworld beneath a disused church, monstrous demons and people being turned inside out. “I woke up and thought: wow!

items.title: Hits, misses and Muppets: the star-studded World Cup half-time show nobody asked for
items.summary: It zipped along, cramming in Shakira, Madonna and Coldplay, but football fans may not be convinced of need for this mid-match spectacleLike hydration breaks and Donald Trump’s ability to get refereeing decisions overturned by simply phoning up the president of Fifa and complaining, the final half-time show is an innovation of the 2026 World Cup that comes with a degree of controversy attached.

items.title: When Maga’s power fades, we cannot abandon those ICE killed | Moira Donegan
items.summary: When this is all over, people will tell us to move on. But those who perpetrated this reign of terror must be held accountableOne day, when Maga is out of power and the conditions of political possibility have changed, there are going to be people who tell you that the best thing for the country is to move on.

items.title: Jennifer Finch, L7 Bassist, Dead At 59
items.summary: Jennifer Finch of L7 has died. The musician joined the Los Angeles punk band in 1986 as bassist and vocalist and played on five of their studio LPs, including 1990’s Smell The Magic and 1992’s Bricks Are Heavy, two of the grunge era’s essential and most influential albums. Earlier this week, Finch revealed she’d been diagnosed with an aggressive form of brain cancer, forcing her to sit out L7’s fall farewell tour.
